codeReview.summary: The commit adds the advertised runtime, hook, reporting, and OIDC surfaces in `core`, but the saved-session bootstrap contract is still too narrow for real session reuse and the handler registry allows silent duplicate overrides.

issues.description: `PlaywrightRuntime.start` always creates the `BrowserContext` before invoking the auth bootstrapper, and the saved-session path only exposes local-storage seeding (`SavedSessionMaterial` / `SavedSessionOidcHandler`). That means a saved-session implementation cannot supply cookies or Playwright storage-state inputs at context-creation time, so app-specific consumers still cannot model real saved-session reuse through the shared core contract. This falls short of the feature requirement to represent saved-session OIDC handlers without pushing consumers to bypass `core`.

The registry constructor uses `byType.put(...)` without detecting duplicates, so registering two handlers for the same `OidcHandlerType` silently keeps the last one. For a shared pluggable auth surface, that makes handler selection order-dependent and hides misconfiguration that should fail fast.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared-state library documents runtime configuration (`core-config.md`) but does not capture the newly added public runtime/auth/reporting entrypoints that later workers will need to consume from `core`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/` currently contains `architecture.md`, `core-config.md`, `environment.md`, and `user-testing.md`, while this feature introduced public APIs in `core/src/main/java/com/example/e2e/core/runtime/PlaywrightRuntime.java`, `core/src/main/java/com/example/e2e/core/runtime/RuntimeHooks.java`, `core/src/main/java/com/example/e2e/core/reporting/ExecutionReporter.java`, and `core/src/main/java/com/example/e2e/core/auth/oidc/PluggableOidcAuthBootstrapper.java`.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, shared-state docs, and commit `ad8a9d56d1d42d798bae7ef86e3fd6c2bb8ae1a3`. The implementation adds substantial reusable `core` runtime infrastructure, but the saved-session OIDC contract cannot yet represent session reuse that must shape browser-context creation, so the feature does not fully satisfy its pluggable saved-session requirement.
